description: 28-case golden dataset for MIRA system testing across 4 scenario zones: Happy Path, Exceptions, Edge Cases, and Adversarial. Each case provides simulated phase 1-3 outputs and a candidate recommendation, with expected eval outcomes to verify the evaluation system itself.

cases.description: One model clearly best, clean data, perfect recommendation. All evals should pass.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset contains 50,000 records with 28 features. Class distribution is well-balanced and data quality is high.

model_building.genai_narrative: Five classification models were evaluated. LightGBM emerged as the top performer.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM and XGBoost passed all quality checks and emerged as top performers.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM demonstrated the strongest ability to identify high-risk customers, correctly classifying them 88% of the time while keeping false alarms at a manageable level.
tradeoffs: Requires more memory than simpler models
Longer training time than Logistic Regression
Needs periodic retraining as customer behavior shifts
next_steps: Deploy in shadow mode alongside existing system for 30 days
Monitor live false positive rate weekly and set alert thresholds
Establish quarterly model retraining schedule with performance benchmarks
deployment_considerations: Requires minimum 8GB RAM for batch scoring
API response time target under 200ms
risks: Model performance may degrade on new customer segments not in training data
Regulatory review required before production deployment
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: After thorough evaluation of five candidate models, LightGBM has been selected as the best fit for deployment. It correctly identifies 88% of high-risk customers with acceptable false alarm rates and has passed all stability and reliability checks. The model generalizes well and shows no signs of memorizing training data. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed to deployment planning.

cases.description: All models perform below the 0.65 AUC threshold. HITL must be triggered.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 15,000 records. Several features show high missingness which may limit model performance.

model_building.genai_narrative: All five models show poor discriminative ability, likely due to low feature informativeness.

model_testing.genai_narrative: No model achieves acceptable performance. Human review is strongly recommended.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: Despite low overall performance, LightGBM marginally outperformed other models and is the least-bad option available.
tradeoffs: Performance is near-random and not suitable for production use
Limited feature informativeness constrains all models
Cost of false negatives may be unacceptably high
next_steps: Conduct a feature engineering sprint to add more predictive signals
Explore additional data sources to improve model inputs
Re-evaluate after data quality improvements
deployment_considerations: Not recommended for deployment in current state
risks: Deploying this model could cause more harm than a rule-based approach
Model performs only marginally better than random chance
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: All models perform below the minimum acceptable threshold. Deployment is not advised.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: None of the five models evaluated show sufficient accuracy to be safely deployed. The best model correctly identifies only 60% of cases, barely better than a coin flip. Deploying any of these models carries significant risk of poor business outcomes. RECOMMENDATION: NO — do not deploy until additional data or features are sourced.

cases.description: 96/4 class split with SMOTE applied. Recommendation correctly highlights imbalance handling.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has a severe class imbalance with only 4% positive cases. SMOTE resampling was applied during model training.

model_building.genai_narrative: SMOTE resampling was applied to address class imbalance. All models were evaluated on the resampled training set.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM performs best on the highly imbalanced dataset, catching the most default cases.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM catches the most loan defaults — identifying approximately 74% of high-risk borrowers correctly, which is the key business priority given the 4% default rate.
tradeoffs: Will still miss 26% of actual defaulters
High-accuracy headline figure is misleading due to class imbalance
Model requires careful threshold tuning to balance catch rate vs. false alarms
next_steps: Set classification threshold to optimize recall over precision for this use case
Review flagged cases manually for the first 90 days post-deployment
Track monthly default catch rate and compare to pre-model baseline
deployment_considerations: Threshold tuning required before go-live
Business team must agree on acceptable false positive rate
risks: Class imbalance may cause performance degradation if default rate changes significantly
Model was trained on SMOTE-augmented data — real-world performance may differ slightly
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM has been selected as the recommended model for loan default detection. Despite the challenging data distribution, it correctly flags approximately 74% of high-risk borrowers — a meaningful improvement over current processes. The team must carefully tune the sensitivity of the system before deployment. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with threshold configuration and shadow deployment.

cases.description: Best model suspected of data leakage. HITL must be triggered as a CRITICAL risk.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 30,000 records. Initial quality checks passed with minor issues.

model_building.genai_narrative: XGBoost achieved suspiciously high performance. Leakage checks flagged during testing.

model_testing.genai_narrative: XGBoost was flagged for suspected data leakage. LightGBM is the recommended alternative.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM is the best-performing model that passed all integrity checks. XGBoost was excluded due to data integrity concerns that would make its impressive numbers unreliable in production.
tradeoffs: Top-performing model was excluded due to reliability concerns
LightGBM performance is strong but not as high as the excluded model appeared
Investigation of data collection process recommended
next_steps: Investigate the data pipeline to identify the source of leakage in XGBoost features
Audit all feature definitions before any deployment
Re-run full model evaluation after leakage source is identified and removed
deployment_considerations: Deployment must wait until leakage investigation is complete
XGBoost should not be deployed under any circumstances without investigation
risks: Unknown leakage source may affect other models indirectly
Incomplete investigation before deployment could expose the organization to unreliable predictions
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: Data leakage was detected in the top-performing model. Human investigation of the data pipeline is required before any deployment decision.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: A data integrity issue was discovered during testing that makes our best-performing model unreliable. LightGBM, the next-best model with clean integrity, has been selected instead. Before deployment, the data team must investigate how the leakage occurred. RECOMMENDATION: NO — halt deployment until data integrity is confirmed.

cases.description: All models within 0.015 AUC — no clear winner. HITL should trigger due to ambiguous selection.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset is well-structured. All five models perform similarly, suggesting the features may be reaching a performance ceiling.

model_building.genai_narrative: All five models perform within 1.5% of each other, making model selection statistically ambiguous.

model_testing.genai_narrative: Models are statistically equivalent. Selection based solely on marginal numeric differences is not advised.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: Given that all models perform equivalently, Logistic Regression is recommended for its simplicity, interpretability, fastest inference time, and easiest regulatory compliance.
tradeoffs: Marginally lower raw performance than complex models
May not capture non-linear patterns as well as tree-based models
Selection relies on operational criteria rather than performance gap
next_steps: Consult with business stakeholders on whether interpretability or performance is the priority
Run A/B test between Logistic Regression and LightGBM in production
Invest in feature engineering to create a more meaningful performance gap
deployment_considerations: All models are operationally equivalent; deployment choice should be driven by infrastructure and compliance requirements
risks: Tied model performance indicates feature ceiling — model may not improve without new data sources
Selection could be challenged in stakeholder review
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: All models perform within 1.5% of each other. The selection is based on operational factors rather than performance. Human judgment is required to confirm the decision criteria.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: Five models were evaluated and all performed at a nearly identical level. Logistic Regression is recommended for its simplicity and ease of explanation to regulators, not because it outperformed others. A human decision-maker should confirm whether this trade-off is acceptable. RECOMMENDATION: NO — defer to stakeholder review before final decision.

cases.description: Recommendation is solid but executive_summary contains technical jargon. Behavior eval must fail no_jargon check.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: High quality dataset with 40,000 balanced records.

model_building.genai_narrative: XGBoost achieved the best performance.

model_testing.genai_narrative: XGBoost and LightGBM are top performers. All models passed testing.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: XGBoost delivered the highest accuracy across all validation folds, consistently outperforming competitors on the holdout set.
tradeoffs: Higher computational cost than LightGBM
Less interpretable than Logistic Regression
Requires more tuning effort
next_steps: Deploy to staging environment for integration testing
Establish monitoring dashboards for prediction drift
Schedule 6-month model review
deployment_considerations: Standard deployment infrastructure sufficient
risks: Performance may degrade on data from regions not represented in training
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: XGBoost has been selected based on its superior ROC-AUC score of 0.86 and high F1 score across validation folds. The model demonstrates strong precision and recall balance suitable for production use. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with deployment.

cases.description: Recommended model shows significant overfitting (train 0.95 vs test 0.70). HITL triggered.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: 18,000 records with 31 features. Dataset is moderately sized.

model_building.genai_narrative: Random Forest shows inflated training performance with high cross-validation variance, suggesting overfitting.

model_testing.genai_narrative: Random Forest was flagged for overfitting. LightGBM is the recommended model.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM consistently outperforms other models on unseen data and shows stable behavior. Random Forest was excluded due to signs of memorizing training data rather than learning generalizable patterns.
tradeoffs: Slightly lower headline accuracy than the excluded Random Forest model
More complex to explain than Logistic Regression
Requires parameter tuning for optimal performance
next_steps: Validate on a held-out time period to confirm temporal stability
Set up automated retraining triggers if performance drops below 0.75
Document model exclusion criteria for audit trail
deployment_considerations: Standard deployment infrastructure sufficient
Monitoring for distribution shift recommended
risks: Dataset is moderately small — performance may fluctuate on edge cases
Feature distribution may shift if business conditions change
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM has been selected after the highest-performing training model was excluded for reliability concerns. LightGBM correctly identifies 81% of target cases on new data it has never seen — a strong and trustworthy result. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with LightGBM deployment.

cases.description: Recommendation output is missing deployment_considerations and risks fields. Completeness checks fail.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: Standard 35,000 record dataset. Balanced classes, clean data.

model_building.genai_narrative: Three models trained. LightGBM leads.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM is the clear top performer.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM demonstrated the best balance of accuracy and reliability across all validation tests.
tradeoffs: Requires more computational resources than simpler alternatives
Less interpretable for regulatory reviews
next_steps: Initiate staging environment deployment
Configure monitoring alerts
Schedule 3-month review
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM is recommended for deployment with strong performance results. RECOMMENDATION: YES.

cases.description: Agent recommends 2nd-best model despite clear winner. Judge accuracy dimension should score low.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: Excellent quality dataset with 55,000 records.

model_building.genai_narrative: LightGBM is the clear leader at 0.91 ROC-AUC, 3 points ahead of XGBoost.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM significantly outperforms all other models.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: XGBoost was chosen for its strong community support, wide deployment tooling ecosystem, and solid performance on this dataset.
tradeoffs: Slightly lower accuracy than LightGBM
Well-supported and widely deployed in industry
Better documentation and monitoring integrations available
next_steps: Deploy XGBoost to staging
Configure SHAP-based explanations for regulatory review
Set performance alerting at 0.80 threshold
deployment_considerations: Mature deployment tooling available for XGBoost
risks: Marginal performance gap vs LightGBM may matter in high-stakes decisions
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: XGBoost has been selected as the production model. It delivers strong performance and has excellent operational tooling for long-term maintenance. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with XGBoost deployment.

expected_eval.notes: Judge accuracy dimension should flag that LightGBM (0.91) was skipped in favor of XGBoost (0.87)

cases.description: Good recommendation but executive_summary has no YES or NO verdict. Behavior eval fails has_deployment_verdict.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: Solid dataset with 42,000 records and clean balanced classes.

model_building.genai_narrative: XGBoost leads with strong cross-validation performance.

model_testing.genai_narrative: XGBoost and LightGBM pass all checks.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: XGBoost provides the strongest ability to correctly identify high-risk customers at 85%, making it the most reliable option for this use case.
tradeoffs: Higher compute cost than LightGBM
Slightly harder to explain to non-technical stakeholders
Requires tuning if data distribution shifts
next_steps: Complete integration testing in staging
Establish baseline metrics for post-deployment monitoring
Train operations team on model outputs and escalation procedures
deployment_considerations: Standard cloud infrastructure is sufficient
Regular monitoring dashboards recommended
risks: Seasonal shifts in customer behavior may reduce model accuracy over time
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: XGBoost has been selected based on its consistent and reliable performance across all validation scenarios. It correctly flags 85% of high-risk customers and has passed all quality and stability checks. The model is considered ready for the next phase of the deployment process pending final stakeholder sign-off.

cases.description: Small but sufficient dataset (2,800 rows) with clean data. LightGBM wins clearly at 0.86 AUC with a 0.07 gap over XGBoost.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset contains 2,800 records with 15 features. Data quality is high with minimal missing values and balanced classes.

model_building.genai_narrative: LightGBM leads by a clear margin of 0.07 AUC over XGBoost. All models passed quality checks.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM is the clear winner. All models passed integrity checks on the test set.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM outperforms all competitors by a meaningful margin, achieving 85% accuracy on unseen data despite the small dataset size. The 7-point gap over XGBoost confirms this is a clear selection.
tradeoffs: Small dataset means confidence intervals are wider than ideal
Performance may be more volatile with future data than on larger datasets
Model will need more frequent retraining as new data arrives
next_steps: Deploy in shadow mode to collect additional production data
Monitor performance closely for the first 60 days post-deployment
Plan a full model refresh once dataset grows past 10,000 records
deployment_considerations: Low memory and compute requirements due to small dataset
Fast inference time suitable for real-time scoring
risks: Small training set may not capture all edge cases in the real population
Performance estimates have higher uncertainty given limited data
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM has been selected based on its strong and consistent performance across all tests, correctly identifying the target outcome 85% of the time. The dataset is small but sufficient, and the model shows no concerning patterns. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed to deployment with enhanced monitoring.

cases.description: Business problem focused on catching fraud; EDA inferred recall as primary metric. Winner has recall=0.82, AUC=0.84.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset contains 38,000 fraud transactions. Business context indicates that missing fraud cases (false negatives) is far more costly than false alarms. Primary metric inferred as recall.

model_building.genai_narrative: LightGBM achieves the highest recall at 0.82, correctly catching 82% of all fraud cases. This aligns with the business priority of minimizing missed fraud.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM catches the most fraud cases. All models passed quality checks.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM catches 82 out of every 100 fraudulent transactions — the highest recall of any tested model. For a fraud detection system where missed cases carry severe financial and reputational consequences, this is the decisive factor.
tradeoffs: Higher false alarm rate than precision-optimized models — operations team will investigate more alerts
Requires threshold tuning to balance workload against missed fraud
Model must be retrained regularly as fraud patterns evolve
next_steps: Work with fraud operations team to agree on acceptable false alarm rate before go-live
Deploy with shadow mode for 30 days to calibrate alert volume
Establish monthly retraining cadence given dynamic fraud patterns
deployment_considerations: Alert triage workflow must be updated to handle increased flagging volume
Real-time scoring required for card-present transactions
risks: Fraud pattern shifts could reduce recall without warning — drift monitoring is essential
High false positive rate may cause alert fatigue in operations teams
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM has been selected as the fraud detection model. It successfully catches 82% of fraudulent transactions — the strongest result across all tested models — and has passed all reliability checks. This model provides a meaningful improvement over current detection capabilities. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with deployment after operations workflow alignment.

cases.description: Gradient Boosting wins at 0.83 AUC with a 0.05 gap over Random Forest. No flags raised. All evals pass.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset contains 29,000 records with clean features and balanced classes. No significant quality issues detected.

model_building.genai_narrative: Gradient Boosting leads by 0.05 AUC over Random Forest. All models are stable with no overfitting detected.

model_testing.genai_narrative: Gradient Boosting is the top performer with a clear 5-point advantage. All models passed quality checks.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: Gradient Boosting consistently achieves the highest performance at 82% on unseen data, with a clear 5-point advantage over the next best model. The model is stable and generalizes well.
tradeoffs: Longer training time compared to LightGBM or Logistic Regression
More difficult to tune hyperparameters than simpler models
Scoring latency higher than LightGBM for batch jobs
next_steps: Deploy to staging for integration validation
Set up SHAP feature importance reports for the operations team
Schedule quarterly retraining with new production data
deployment_considerations: Adequate compute required for batch scoring; real-time latency is acceptable
Model artifacts require versioning for audit trail
risks: Performance may decline if feature distributions shift over time
Longer training time means retraining cycles take more compute budget
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: Gradient Boosting has been selected as the recommended model. It achieves the highest accuracy on unseen data and has passed all reliability and stability checks. No quality flags were raised during evaluation. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed to deployment planning.

cases.description: Simple linearly separable dataset. Logistic Regression wins at 0.79 AUC with a 0.06 gap. Interpretable model preferred.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset contains 18,500 records with 10 well-structured features. Feature correlations suggest a largely linear decision boundary.

model_building.genai_narrative: Logistic Regression outperforms all tree-based models, indicating the data is largely linearly separable. A 0.06 gap over LightGBM confirms this is a clear recommendation.

model_testing.genai_narrative: Logistic Regression is the top performer. The dataset shows linear separability that benefits simpler models.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: Logistic Regression outperforms all more complex models on this dataset, achieving 79% on unseen data with a clear 6-point advantage. The linear structure of this data favors simpler approaches, which also have the benefit of being easier to explain to business stakeholders and regulators.
tradeoffs: Will not adapt well if the decision boundary becomes non-linear in the future
Cannot capture interaction effects between features without manual feature engineering
Model coefficients require expert interpretation to communicate to non-technical audiences
next_steps: Generate coefficient table for regulatory documentation
Validate model output on a held-out time slice before full deployment
Set up monthly performance review using a held-out validation set
deployment_considerations: Extremely low inference latency suitable for real-time scoring
Easy to retrain and audit — favorable for regulated environments
risks: If data relationships become non-linear, this model will underperform tree-based alternatives
Business rule changes could invalidate linear assumptions
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: Logistic Regression has been selected as the recommended model. It outperforms all tested alternatives, is fast, interpretable, and easy to maintain. The data structure supports this choice. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with deployment.

cases.description: Balanced classes with EDA inferring f1_score as primary metric. Winner has f1=0.81, AUC=0.85. All evals pass.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 33,000 records with well-balanced classes. Business context requires balanced precision and recall, so F1-score has been selected as the primary metric.

model_building.genai_narrative: XGBoost achieves the highest F1 score at 0.81, reflecting strong balance between catching true positives and minimizing false alarms.

model_testing.genai_narrative: XGBoost achieves the best balance of precision and recall. All models passed quality checks.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: XGBoost achieves the best balance between identifying true cases and avoiding false alarms, with an F1 score of 0.81. This aligns with the business requirement to treat both types of errors as equally costly.
tradeoffs: Higher training and scoring cost than LightGBM
Less interpretable than simpler models for regulatory reporting
Requires hyperparameter tuning to maintain performance over time
next_steps: Validate F1 score on a held-out time slice before production
Establish alert thresholds based on acceptable false positive and false negative rates
Set up automated retraining pipeline triggered by F1 score degradation
deployment_considerations: Scoring infrastructure must support batch and real-time modes
Model versioning required for compliance audit trail
risks: Balanced metric optimization may not hold if class proportions shift in production
Model may require threshold adjustment if business priorities change
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: XGBoost has been selected as the recommended model based on its strong balance between correctly catching target events and avoiding unnecessary alerts. It achieves an F1 score of 0.81 and has passed all quality and stability checks. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed to deployment planning.

cases.description: 8% minority class with class_imbalance_handled=True (balanced weighting applied). LightGBM wins at 0.81 AUC. No flags.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 45,000 records with 8% minority class. Balanced class weighting was applied to all models to address the imbalance. This is a known handling strategy and does not require HITL escalation.

model_building.genai_narrative: Balanced class weighting was applied. LightGBM achieves 0.81 AUC with decent recall at 0.74, correctly handling the imbalanced scenario.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM is the top performer. Balanced weighting handled the minority class effectively. No quality flags raised.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM achieves the highest accuracy at 81% while correctly catching 74% of minority class cases. Balanced weighting was applied throughout training, ensuring the model is not biased toward the majority class.
tradeoffs: Higher false positive rate than an unbalanced model would produce
Accuracy metric alone would be misleading — recall is the key indicator of success here
Model requires threshold calibration to match operational tolerance
next_steps: Tune classification threshold in coordination with operations team
Deploy in shadow mode for 45 days to measure real-world minority class detection rate
Establish monthly review of minority class capture rate as a KPI
deployment_considerations: Balanced weighting setting must be preserved in production scoring pipeline
Alert volumes should be reviewed before full launch
risks: If minority class rate drops further, recall may degrade despite high AUC
Seasonal shifts in class distribution require drift monitoring
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM has been selected as the recommended model. The class imbalance was correctly addressed through balanced weighting, and the model successfully catches 74% of minority class cases with a strong overall score of 81%. All quality checks passed. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with threshold calibration and deployment.

cases.description: Large dataset with 120,000 rows and 45 features. XGBoost wins at 0.91 AUC with 0.04 gap. All evals pass.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset contains 120,000 records with 45 features. High sample size gives strong statistical confidence in all results. Data quality is excellent.

model_building.genai_narrative: XGBoost leads at 0.91 AUC with a 4-point gap over LightGBM. Large dataset provides high statistical confidence in these results.

model_testing.genai_narrative: XGBoost is the clear leader. High sample size makes statistical conclusions robust. All models passed quality checks.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: XGBoost achieves 91% on a 120,000 record dataset, a result with very high statistical confidence. The 4-point gap over LightGBM is consistent across all cross-validation folds, confirming this is a robust selection.
tradeoffs: Longer training time than LightGBM — relevant for retraining cadence planning
Higher memory footprint at inference time on large feature sets
More hyperparameters to manage compared to simpler models
next_steps: Deploy to staging for integration testing with full feature pipeline
Establish real-time monitoring dashboard with hourly score distribution tracking
Schedule automated retraining every 90 days using the most recent data window
deployment_considerations: High-volume scoring requires adequate compute cluster — estimate 4-8 cores for batch jobs
Model artifacts should be stored in versioned model registry
risks: High performance may mask subtle distribution drift — monitoring is critical even for well-performing models
Feature pipeline complexity with 45 features requires careful dependency management
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: XGBoost has been selected as the recommended model. With 120,000 training records and a test score of 91%, this is one of the strongest results in our evaluation suite. The model is stable, generalizes well, and has passed all quality checks. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed to deployment.

cases.description: AUC=0.73, above the 0.65 floor. Business problem does not require high precision. Clear winner with 0.06 gap. All evals pass.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 21,000 records with 14 features. Data quality is acceptable. Business context indicates that moderate model accuracy is sufficient for this low-stakes use case.

model_building.genai_narrative: Random Forest achieves a moderate but acceptable 0.73 AUC with a clear 0.06 gap over LightGBM. Performance is above the minimum threshold for this use case.

model_testing.genai_narrative: Random Forest is the top performer. Performance is moderate but above the acceptable threshold for this low-stakes use case.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: Random Forest achieves 73% — above the minimum acceptable threshold and with a clear 6-point lead over the next best model. For a low-stakes prioritization use case where decisions are reversible, this performance level is sufficient.
tradeoffs: Performance is moderate — not suitable for high-stakes irreversible decisions
Cannot capture complex non-linear patterns as well as boosting models on richer datasets
Will benefit from expanded feature engineering if higher accuracy is needed in the future
next_steps: Deploy with human review layer for borderline predictions (probability 0.40-0.60)
Track model accuracy monthly against a held-out validation set
Revisit with additional feature engineering after 6 months
deployment_considerations: Human review workflow recommended for edge cases given moderate accuracy
Low compute requirements make deployment straightforward
risks: Moderate accuracy means some errors are expected — business process must account for this
Performance may not generalize to significantly different populations
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: Random Forest has been selected as the recommended model. While performance is moderate at 73%, it exceeds the minimum acceptable threshold and provides a meaningful improvement over no model. The business context supports deployment at this performance level. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed with deployment and human review layer for borderline cases.

cases.description: EDA inferred precision as primary metric because false positives are costly. Winner has precision=0.84, AUC=0.82. All evals pass.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 26,000 records. Business context indicates false positives are highly costly — each incorrect flag triggers an expensive manual review. Primary metric has been inferred as precision.

model_building.genai_narrative: LightGBM achieves the highest precision at 0.84, meaning 84% of its positive predictions are correct. This directly minimizes costly false alarm reviews.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM achieves the highest precision. All models passed quality checks.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM achieves a precision of 0.84, meaning 84 out of every 100 flagged cases are genuine — the highest rate of any tested model. This directly reduces the number of costly false positive reviews, which is the primary business concern.
tradeoffs: Higher precision means slightly lower recall — some genuine cases will be missed
Threshold tuning can shift the precision-recall balance if requirements change
Periodic review of missed cases is needed to assess whether recall is acceptably low
next_steps: Calibrate classification threshold to the target precision level agreed with operations
Implement a secondary review layer for borderline predictions to recover missed cases
Monitor precision on a weekly basis post-deployment
deployment_considerations: Real-time scoring pipeline must preserve threshold setting
Operations team must be briefed on expected false negative rate
risks: If distribution shifts, precision may degrade — requiring threshold recalibration
Missed cases (false negatives) must be tracked and reported to stakeholders
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM has been selected as the recommended model. It achieves the highest precision among all evaluated models, correctly identifying 84% of flagged cases and minimizing costly false alarms. All quality and stability checks passed. RECOMMENDATION: YES — proceed to deployment with threshold calibration.

cases.description: Stability flag triggered for all models (cv_std > 0.05). Winner has AUC=0.80 but HITL triggered due to instability.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 24,000 records but all models exhibit high variance across cross-validation folds, suggesting unstable learning behavior. This may be caused by noisy labels or inconsistent data collection.

model_building.genai_narrative: All five models show cross-validation standard deviation above 0.05, indicating high performance variance. Results cannot be relied upon for stable production performance.

model_testing.genai_narrative: All models flagged for high cross-validation variance. Human review is required before any deployment decision.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM achieves the highest score at 80%, but all models including this one show concerning performance variance across validation folds. This instability makes reliable production performance uncertain.
tradeoffs: High cross-validation variance means test set performance may not reflect real-world results
All alternatives show similar or worse instability
Root cause of variance must be investigated before deployment
next_steps: Investigate label quality — noisy labels are a common cause of high model variance
Review data collection process for inconsistencies across time periods
Consider data cleaning or augmentation before re-running model selection
deployment_considerations: Deployment is not recommended until variance is resolved
Human expert review of data pipeline required
risks: Unstable model may produce unpredictable results in production
Performance variance suggests the model may not generalize reliably to unseen data
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: All models exhibit high cross-validation variance (cv_std > 0.05), indicating unstable learning behavior. Production deployment is not advisable without human investigation of the root cause.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: While LightGBM shows the strongest performance at 80%, all models in this evaluation show significant instability across validation folds. This raises serious concerns about reliable production performance. A human expert must investigate the data pipeline before any deployment decision is made. RECOMMENDATION: NO — do not deploy until stability issues are resolved.

cases.description: 4% minority class with class_imbalance_handled=False. Imbalance was not corrected. HITL triggered.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has a severe 4% minority class. Class imbalance handling was not applied during model training. High accuracy scores are misleading as models may simply predict the majority class.

model_building.genai_narrative: All models show high accuracy but very low recall — a sign of imbalance not being handled. Models are likely predicting the majority class for most cases.

model_testing.genai_narrative: All models are failing to detect the minority class. Recall rates of 8-19% are unacceptable for any classification task where the minority class matters.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: XGBoost achieves the highest overall score but only catches 19% of minority class cases — a critically low recall due to unhandled class imbalance. Human review is required.
tradeoffs: Recall of 19% means 81% of target cases are completely missed by this model
High accuracy is deceptive — the model is primarily predicting the majority class
All models suffer from the same fundamental flaw
next_steps: Re-run all model training with SMOTE oversampling or balanced class weighting
Investigate why imbalance handling was not applied in the original pipeline
Do not deploy any model from this evaluation without imbalance correction
deployment_considerations: No model from this evaluation should be deployed
Pipeline must be corrected before re-evaluation
risks: Deploying this model would miss 81% of all target cases — a critical failure for any operational use case
High accuracy headlines would give false confidence to stakeholders
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: Severe class imbalance (4% minority) was detected but not corrected during training. All models show critically low recall. The pipeline must be fixed and models re-trained before any deployment decision.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: A critical flaw was identified in the model training pipeline — class imbalance was not addressed, causing all models to fail at detecting the minority class. The best model only catches 19% of target cases, which is insufficient for any practical use. Deployment is not possible in the current state. RECOMMENDATION: NO — halt evaluation and fix the imbalance handling pipeline.

cases.description: Overfitting gap of 0.11 (just over threshold). HITL triggered. Model still recommended but with explicit flag.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 20,000 records. The best model shows a training-to-test performance gap of 0.11, which exceeds the acceptable overfitting threshold of 0.10.

model_building.genai_narrative: LightGBM has the highest raw score but an overfitting gap of 0.11, just over the 0.10 threshold. This triggers a quality flag and HITL escalation.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM was flagged for borderline overfitting. XGBoost is now the top clean model at 0.79 on the test set.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM has the highest training score, but its overfitting gap of 0.11 is just above the acceptable threshold. It is being flagged for human review while still being noted as the highest-potential model pending regularization tuning.
tradeoffs: Overfitting gap of 0.11 exceeds the 0.10 threshold — generalization reliability is uncertain
XGBoost achieves 0.79 on the test set without any overfitting flag and may be the safer choice
Regularization tuning of LightGBM could resolve the gap without losing performance
next_steps: Apply stronger regularization (increase lambda, reduce max depth) and re-evaluate LightGBM
If regularization does not close the gap, switch recommendation to XGBoost
Human expert must confirm whether overfitting gap is acceptable given the business context
deployment_considerations: Do not deploy LightGBM without first applying regularization adjustments
XGBoost is the safer immediate deployment option pending human review
risks: Deploying with 0.11 overfitting gap may result in worse-than-expected real-world performance
Gap may widen on production data with different distribution than test set
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: The recommended model has an overfitting gap of 0.11, just above the 0.10 threshold. A human expert should confirm whether this is acceptable or whether XGBoost should be selected instead.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: LightGBM shows the highest potential but a borderline overfitting pattern that requires human review before deployment. XGBoost is a safe alternative with clean test performance. A qualified expert must decide whether to proceed with LightGBM after regularization tuning or switch to XGBoost immediately. RECOMMENDATION: NO — human review required before deployment decision.

cases.description: Top feature accounts for 72% of importance, suspicious of latent leakage. HITL triggered. production_ready=false.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 31,000 records. Feature importance analysis reveals that a single feature (primary_score) accounts for 72% of the model's decision-making. This concentration is suspicious and may indicate latent data leakage or an overly engineered proxy feature.

model_building.genai_narrative: All models heavily rely on primary_score. XGBoost assigns 72% of its importance to this single feature. This concentration pattern raises concerns about feature validity and potential leakage.

model_testing.genai_narrative: All tree-based models flagged for feature concentration risk. Leakage via primary_score cannot be ruled out without further investigation.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: XGBoost achieves the highest score at 86%, but this performance may be inflated by a single feature (primary_score) that accounts for 72% of model importance. This concentration pattern requires expert review before any deployment.
tradeoffs: 72% feature concentration in a single predictor is a major red flag for latent leakage
Strong headline performance may be unreliable if primary_score is removed or changes in production
Logistic Regression is the only model without concentration concern, but performs significantly lower
next_steps: Audit the definition and construction of primary_score for potential leakage
Re-run all models with primary_score excluded to assess true predictive power of remaining features
If primary_score is validated as clean, reconsider deployment with documentation
deployment_considerations: Do not deploy until primary_score audit is complete
If feature is validated, consider building monitoring specifically for primary_score drift
risks: If primary_score is a proxy for the target variable, model performance will collapse in production
High concentration in a single feature creates fragility — any change to that feature breaks the model
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: Top feature accounts for 72% of model importance across all tree-based models. This pattern is suspicious of latent leakage or an invalid proxy feature. Human expert audit of feature construction is required.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: XGBoost shows strong performance, but all tree-based models are dominated by a single feature that may not be valid for production use. A human expert must audit this feature before any deployment decision. RECOMMENDATION: NO — halt deployment pending feature audit.

cases.description: Winner AUC exactly 0.66, just above 0.65 floor. Low confidence score (~0.62) triggers HITL. Recommendation correctly notes borderline status.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: The dataset has 12,000 records but shows low feature informativeness. The best model AUC is exactly 0.66 — just at the minimum acceptable floor. EDA confidence is low at 0.62 due to data quality concerns.

model_building.genai_narrative: Random Forest barely clears the minimum threshold at 0.66. All other models fall at or below the floor. Performance is borderline and results may not be reliable given data quality issues.

model_testing.genai_narrative: Random Forest is the only model at or above the 0.65 floor. Performance is borderline. Human review recommended given the narrow margin and low EDA confidence.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: Random Forest is the only model to clear the minimum acceptable threshold of 0.65 AUC, achieving exactly 0.66. However, this margin is razor-thin and the overall data quality concerns make this a borderline recommendation requiring human validation.
tradeoffs: Performance is at the absolute minimum acceptable level — any real-world variation could push it below the floor
Data quality issues (high missingness, low feature informativeness) mean results may not generalize well
All alternative models fall below the minimum threshold
next_steps: Conduct data quality improvement sprint before any deployment decision
Re-evaluate with cleaner data — particularly addressing missingness in score_2
Human expert must confirm whether 0.66 AUC is acceptable for this specific business context
deployment_considerations: Deployment at this performance level is only defensible for very low-stakes decisions
Enhanced monitoring required if deployed — any performance dip would push below minimum
risks: Real-world performance may fall below the minimum threshold given the narrow margin
Low feature informativeness suggests model may not generalize to new data patterns
candidate_recommendation.human_review_reason: Winner AUC of 0.66 is only marginally above the 0.65 floor. Combined with low EDA confidence (0.62) and data quality issues, a human expert must assess whether deployment is appropriate.
candidate_recommendation.executive_summary: Random Forest is the only model to clear the minimum performance threshold, but only barely. Given the borderline result and underlying data quality concerns, this evaluation cannot confidently recommend deployment. A qualified expert must assess the business risk tolerance before proceeding. RECOMMENDATION: NO — human review required given borderline performance.

cases.description: Models report impossible values: roc_auc=1.05 and cv_std=-0.02. Recommendation fails to flag these. Eval system must catch.

data_exploration.genai_narrative: Standard dataset with 28,000 records. Model evaluation produced anomalous metric values that were not caught by the pipeline.

model_building.genai_narrative: LightGBM reports an impossible roc_auc of 1.05 and a negative cv_std of -0.02. These values are mathematically impossible and indicate a pipeline error. The recommendation agent did not flag these anomalies.

model_testing.genai_narrative: LightGBM was selected as top model despite impossible metric values. The pipeline failed to catch the anomaly.

candidate_recommendation.selection_reason: LightGBM achieves the highest ROC-AUC score of 1.05, outperforming all other models by a significant margin. This exceptional result makes it the clear choice for deployment.
tradeoffs: Excellent performance across all metrics
May require additional compute for batch scoring
Periodic retraining recommended
next_steps: Deploy LightGBM to production immediately
Set up monitoring dashboards
Schedule quarterly reviews

risks: None identified — model performance is exceptional

model_testing.genai_narrative: All models fail minimum performance threshold. Multiple quality flags raised across overfitting, leakage, and stability dimensions. This evaluation must not produce a high-confidence recommendation.
